item.dc:creator: Qi Gao, Wei Chen

item.title: [7] Essential Principles and Practices in X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy
item.description:
Last authors: Jan \v{C}echal

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Overview of X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy (XPS), a related photoemission technique.

Tags: #XPS #photoemission #spectroscopy

Abstract: arXiv:2512.24756v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) is a widely used technique for chemical analysis of solid surfaces, sensitive to the chemical environments of atoms via core-level binding energy shifts. While modern instruments allow experimental data to be acquired with ease, their evaluation and interpretation remain challenging for newcomers to the field, as a profound knowledge of the method is required for correct analysis. Here we present a concise yet comprehensive overview of the fundamental principles and methodologies of XPS, covering photoemission processes, chemical shifts, charge referencing, peak fitting, and quantification strategies. This overview aims to bridge the gap between data collection and reliable analysis, providing essential knowledge for correct interpretation. By clarifying key concepts and common practices, this work supports improved accuracy in surface chemical characterization using XPS.
item.dc:creator: Jan \v{C}echal

item.title: [8] Intervalley Band Crossing and Transition of Fractional Chern Insulators in Floquet Twisted Bilayer MoTe2
item.description:
Last authors: Yuhao Shi, Zhao Liu

Score 8/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Floquet engineering and fractional Chern insulators in twisted bilayer MoTe2, including TRSB, highly relevant theory.; tier capped to C: formal/generic theory watch

Tags: #Floquet #twistedbilayer #MoTe2 #Cherninsulator #time-reversalsymmetrybreaking #topological

Abstract: arXiv:2601.03104v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We study the twisted MoTe$_2$ homobilayer coupled to periodic driving of a circularly polarized light (CPL). Using Floquet theory in the high-frequency limit, we start from the Dirac model including both the valence and conduction bands of monolayer MoTe$_2$ to derive an effective time-independent Floquet Hamiltonian. The photon processes coupling the valence and conduction bands are captured in this Floquet analysis, and the resulting Floquet Hamiltonian contains explicit time-reversal symmetry breaking terms that are absent if conduction bands are integrated out from the beginning of the derivation. Based on the Floquet Hamiltonian, we find the increase of CPL driving intensity can cause the crossing of Floquet bands and redistribution of holes between the two valleys. When interactions are included, a transition between Floquet Laughlin-type FCIs with different behaviors of valley polarization is identified at total hole filling $5/3$.
item.dc:creator: Yuhao Shi, Zhao Liu

item.title: [9] Lattice fermion simulation of spontaneous time-reversal symmetry breaking in a helical Luttinger liquid
item.description:
Last authors: J. S\'anchez Fern\'an, C. W. J. Beenakker

Authors: V. A. Zakharov, J. S\'anchez Fern\'an, C. W. J. Beenakker

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: title strong match: Luttinger liquid

Tags: #time-reversalsymmetrybreaking #Dirac #Luttinger

Abstract: arXiv:2601.09563v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We extend a recently developed "tangent fermion" method to discretize the Hamiltonian of a helical Luttinger liquid on a one-dimensional lattice, including two-particle backscattering processes that may open a gap in the spectrum. The fermion-doubling obstruction of the sine dispersion is avoided by working with a tangent dispersion, preserving the time-reversal symmetry of the Hamiltonian. The numerical results from a tensor network calculation on a finite lattice confirm the expectation from infinite-system analytics, that a gapped phase with spontaneously broken time-reversal symmetry emerges when the Fermi level is tuned to the Dirac point and the Luttinger parameter crosses a critical value.
item.dc:creator: V. A. Zakharov, J. S\'anchez Fern\'an, C. W. J. Beenakker

item.title: [8] Magneto-optical transport in type-II Weyl semimetals in the presence of orbital magnetic moment
item.description:
Last authors: Panchlal Prabhat, Amit Gupta

Score 8/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Theory of magneto-optical transport in type-II Weyl semimetals, highly relevant to topological materials.; tier capped to C: formal/generic theory watch

Tags: #Weylsemimetal #topological #transport #magneto-optical

Abstract: arXiv:2602.04781v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: The magneto-optical transport of gapless type-I tilted single Weyl semimetals(WSMs) exhibits suppression of total magnetoconductivities in the presence of orbital magnetic moment(OMM) in linear and nonlinear responses (Yang Gao et al., Phys. Rev. B {\bf 105}, 165307 (2022)). In this work, we extend our study to investigate magnetoconductivities in gapless type-II Weyl semimetals within the semiclassical Boltzmann approach and show the differences that arise compared to type-I Weyl semimetals.
item.dc:creator: Panchlal Prabhat, Amit Gupta

item.title: [9] Unified theory of orientation averaging in X-ray spectroscopies: understanding polarization dependence in a Cartesian tensor approach
item.description:
Last authors: Marius Retegan, Pieter Glatzel

Authors: Sihan Zhang, Oana Bun\u{a}u, Marius Retegan, Pieter Glatzel

Score 9/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Unified theory for interpreting RIXS experimental data, directly relevant to a key technique.; tier capped to C: formal/generic theory watch

Tags: #RIXS #spectroscopy #methodology #electronicstructure

Abstract: arXiv:2603.12355v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: X-ray absorption spectroscopy (XAS) and resonant inelastic X-ray scattering (RIXS) are powerful probes of electronic structure owing to their chemical and orbital selectivity. For powder samples, however, interpreting RIXS spectral intensities remains challenging as the measured signal is an average over all orientations. Existing theoretical treatments rely largely on spherical-tensor formalisms, which often involve complex derivations and case-specific analyses. Meanwhile, recent advances in quantum-chemistry methods have made the evaluation of transition tensors in Cartesian coordinates both accurate and straightforward. Here, we present a general theoretical framework that translates Cartesian transition tensors into physically meaningful, orientation-averaged intensities for powder samples. The formalism allows predicting angular and polarization dependences \textit{ab initio} for both XAS and RIXS and is extendable to other spectroscopies. The resulting predictions show excellent agreement with RIXS experimental data at the Ce L$_3$ edge.
item.dc:creator: Sihan Zhang, Oana Bun\u{a}u, Marius Retegan, Pieter Glatzel

item.title: [9] Jahn-Teller distortion on strained La3Ni2O7 thin films
item.description:
Last authors: Fu-Chun Zhang, Kun Jiang

Authors: Yuxin Wang, Zhan Wang, Fu-Chun Zhang, Kun Jiang

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: ARPES, nickelate superconductivity, Fermi surface, and electronic structure are highly relevant.

Tags: #ARPES #superconductivity #nickelates #electronicstructure #Fermisurface

Abstract: arXiv:2604.02191v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We present a systematic study of the electronic structure of strained La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ thin films. We show that biaxial compressive strain mainly elongates the outer apical Ni-O bond while leaving the inner apical Ni-O bond nearly unchanged. As a result, the Jahn-Teller splitting $\Delta_{JT}$ is strongly enhanced, whereas the interlayer $d_{z^2}$ hopping $t_\perp^z$ changes only weakly. Since superconductivity is widely believed to emerge only below a critical in-plane lattice constant, our results identify the strain-enhanced $\Delta_{JT}$ as the relevant microscopic tuning parameter. Consistently, the calculated Fermi surfaces and Hall response for LaAlO$_3$ and SrLaAlO$_4$ substrates agree with ARPES and Hall measurements. Our results identify Jahn-Teller distortion as a key tuning parameter in strained La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ and support its central role in optimizing superconductivity in bilayer nickelates.
item.dc:creator: Yuxin Wang, Zhan Wang, Fu-Chun Zhang, Kun Jiang

item.title: [8] Vacancy-driven inverse Lieb geometry: A general route to d-wave altermagnetism in two dimensions
item.description:
Last authors: Katsunori Wakabayashi, Sasmita Mohakud

Authors: Geethanjali S, Katsunori Wakabayashi, Sasmita Mohakud

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Altermagnetism in 2D materials, Fermi surface, and TRSB are highly relevant.

Tags: #altermagnetism #2Dmaterials #monolayer #Fermisurface #TRSB

Abstract: arXiv:2604.10768v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Vacancy-induced structural reconstruction provides a general microscopic route to $d$-wave altermagnetism in two-dimensional systems. As a concrete realization, reconstructed $\mathrm{V_2X_2}$ ($\mathrm{X}=\mathrm{S}, \mathrm{Se}$) monolayers form an inverse Lieb magnetic network in which two inequivalent edge vanadium sites, related by $C_4$ lattice rotational symmetry and carrying opposite exchange fields, yield zero net magnetization despite broken time-reversal ($\mathcal{T}$) and combined inversion--time-reversal ($\mathcal{PT}$) symmetries. Structural stability is confirmed by formation energies, phonon spectra, and $ab$ $ initio$ molecular dynamics simulations at room temperature. A minimal tight-binding model, incorporating anisotropic second-order hopping between the inequivalent magnetic sites mediated by a nonmagnetic corner site, produces spin splitting with a $(\cos k_x - \cos k_y)$ form factor in quantitative agreement with first-principles calculations. The resulting spin splitting is strongly anisotropic, maximized near the $X$ and $Y$ high-symmetry points and exhibiting a symmetry-enforced nodal degeneracy at $M$, consistent with a $d_{x^2-y^2}$ altermagnetic form factor confirmed by the fourfold Fermi surface pattern. These findings establish vacancy-driven reconstruction of an inverse Lieb magnetic network as a general design principle for two-dimensional $d$-wave altermagnets.
item.dc:creator: Geethanjali S, Katsunori Wakabayashi, Sasmita Mohakud

item.title: [7] Strong coupling between quantized magnon modes in a YIG microstucture and microwaves in a superconducting resonator
item.description:
Last authors: Michael Flatt\'e, Georg Schmidt

Authors: Seth W. Kurfman, Philipp Geyer, Anoop Kamalasanan, et al.

Score 7/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Experimental work on magnon-superconductor coupling in quantum materials.; tier capped to C: formal/generic theory watch

Tags: #magnons #superconductivity #quantummaterials

Abstract: arXiv:2604.28145v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Strong-coupling experiments based on magnons enable the exploration into on-chip demonstrations involving numerous long-lived excitations. Yttrium iron garnet (YIG) has been considered for decades as a gold standard material for magnonics due to its low-loss magnonic properties. While YIG has successfully demonstrated strong-coupling in macroscopic device geometries, the strong coupling of magnons in truly sub-10 micron YIG structures to date has not yet been realized. This obstacle is due to the difficulty producing large enough effective magnonic mode volume necessary primarily due to thickness limitations of YIG deposition and device fabrication techniques. Here, we demonstrate the use of a microplatelet of YIG, manufactured from a single crystal of YIG via focused ion beam (FIB) techniques, placed on a constricted inductive line of an optimized superconducting lumped element LC resonator to achieve strong coupling between numerous magnon modes and the LC resonator photons. These experimental findings are qualitatively backed by micromagnetic simulations and quantitatively supported by analytical calculations to identify the magnon modes corresponding to the experimentally observed anti-crossings in the microwave transmission signal. Further, we show that these anti-crossings remain even at incredibly low device input powers ($\leq 10$ fW). The fabrication techniques and device geometry enable the deterministic use of numerous confined magnon modes in micron-scale YIG structures for various magnetic field strengths and orientations at substantially reduced device powers. The results here establish a foundational path forward to achieving efficient magnon-based strong-coupling experiments in micron-scale YIG magnetic elements for effective on-chip studies.
item.dc:creator: Seth W. Kurfman, Philipp Geyer, Anoop Kamalasanan, et al.
